suppress.notes: FALSE POSITIVE (verified 2026-06-11; pending Security-Lead confirmation). CVE-2026-42154 is an unauthenticated DoS in the PROMETHEUS SERVER's remote-read endpoint (/api/v1/read) — the Go project github.com/prometheus/prometheus, fixed in Prometheus 3.5.3 / 3.11.3 (ref: nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-42154). This service depends on io.micrometer:micrometer-registry-prometheus, the Java metrics-EXPOSITION client that serves /actuator/prometheus for scraping. It implements NO remote-read endpoint and contains none of the vulnerable Prometheus-server code. dependency-check mis-attributes the server CVE to the similarly-named client via fuzzy CPE matching; no micrometer release (incl. latest 1.17.0) will ever "fix" a Prometheus-server CVE. Scoped to this package + CVE only.

FALSE POSITIVE (verified 2026-06-12; pending Security-Lead confirmation). CVE-2025-7962 is an SMTP-injection (CRLF) vulnerability in Jakarta Mail / the org.eclipse.angus:angus-mail (a.k.a. :smtp) component, fixed in 2.0.4 (ref: GHSA-9342-92gg-6v29, nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-7962). dependency-check mis-attributes it to org.eclipse.angus:angus-activation — the Jakarta Activation (MIME-type) implementation, which contains no SMTP/mail code — a documented dependency-check false positive (DependencyCheck issue #8144). domain-service pulls angus-activation transitively for MIME handling and does NOT send mail. Scoped to this package + CVE only.

FALSE POSITIVE — mis-assigned CPE (PENDING Security-Lead confirmation). dependency-check assigns the broad SDK CPE cpe:/a:opentelemetry:opentelemetry to the JAVA artifact io.opentelemetry.semconv:opentelemetry-semconv (pulled transitively by micrometer-tracing-bridge-otel, Spring-Boot-BOM-managed), so every OpenTelemetry-GO CVE matches it. opentelemetry-semconv is a CONSTANTS-ONLY artifact — generated semantic-convention attribute keys (e.g. "http.request.method"), no runtime, network, command-exec, parsing, or deserialization code — so none of these Go-SDK CVEs apply. Confirmed Go-only CVEs currently mis-attributed (all in go.opentelemetry.io/otel, fixed in opentelemetry-go v1.43.0): - CVE-2026-24051 — PATH hijacking / arbitrary code execution (Darwin ioreg, BSD kenv); GHSA-9h8m-3fm2-qjrq - CVE-2026-39882 — OTLP-HTTP exporter unbounded-body DoS / memory exhaustion; GHSA-w8rr-5gcm-pp58 - CVE-2026-39883 — BSD/Solaris kenv PATH-hijacking follow-up; GHSA-hfvc-g4fc-pqhx Suppressing the mis-assigned CPE (not per-CVE) so newly-disclosed OTel-Go CVEs — e.g. CVE-2026-24051, which slipped past the previous per-CVE list — do not re-trigger against this constants-only jar. Same fuzzy-CPE pattern as the micrometer/angus entries above. Scoped to this package + CPE only; genuine Java CVEs remain unaffected.
